title: HTTPS, TLS Identity, Certificate Trust, Proxies, and Downgrade Boundaries · Go Client Systems · Go Semantics Case: Https Identity
summary: An internal control-plane client uses private roots and optional mTLS through an audited proxy without disabling ordinary certificate validation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go https identity increment in a Go 1.26 net/http transport trust and certificate evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Defend against decompression bombs, oversized structured values, deep nesting, and unbounded field counts. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
